notes: Spec asserts: a long-idle mistral-vibe session stays alive past readyTTL, state remains ready during the idle window, and the next user prompt resumes ready->working. vibe is a presession adapter, but — matching the codex sibling's long-idle shape — the birth is modeled UNPINNED (session_birth anchored to 'start') so a transient proc-<PID> presession OR the reconciled real session_<ts>_<hash> row (whichever the process scanner mints first) satisfies it, and the turn/idle phases anchor forward so the matcher lands the working transitions on the live session. Persistence is the daemon's adapter-agnostic PID-liveness skip: sweepStaleSnapshot (pid_manager.go:942-948) returns early when snap.pid>0 && Kill(pid,0)==nil, so the idle-past-TTL session is not deleted while the vibe process is alive; if that regresses, the idle_window invariants fire (transcript_removed for the primary session, or a flicker off ready). Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so the 15000ms duration_at_least_ms cleanly exceeds the TTL in ~45s. BACKFLOW: the offline replay golden CANNOT reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so they are UNVERIFIABLE offline — the observation vector asserts neither model nor tokens and matches kiro-cli (agent only, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false). RE-RECORDED 2026-07-13 (issue #988 worktree daemon, irrlichd 0.5.7+2c97cfa, which carries #905's force-bounce fix): the ORIGINALLY diagnosed mechanism (a spurious ready->working->ready flicker firing ~2s after EACH of two distinctly-observed turn_done events) is GONE — no phantom bounce follows any genuine turn_done in this recording. known_failing PERSISTS for a DISTINCT, still-unfixed mechanism: session-discovery latency. The presession proc-<PID> bound ready at +118ms as usual, but the daemon did not discover the real session_<ts>_<hash> transcript file (transcript_new) until +17.3s after the presession bind — by which point vibe's FIRST turn had already fully completed and flushed both its user and assistant lines to messages.jsonl (the file's discovered size, 534 bytes, exactly matches turn 1's content). The daemon's file-driven catch-up on first discovery does not itself emit a working transition for that already-complete turn 1 (it settles the session straight into ready), so the matcher's ONLY observed working->ready cycle is turn 2's, misattributed by the matcher as first_turn_start/first_turn_end (3/6 phases pass: session_birth, first_turn_start, first_turn_end all match). idle_window then FAILS its 'no transcript_removed for primary session' invariant: the recipe's post-verification teardown exits the vibe process ~5s after that (mis-slotted) first_turn_end, well short of the 15s window, cascading second_turn_start/second_turn_end to 'unknown anchor' failures. Root cause is plausibly this dev machine's large pre-existing session backlog under real $HOME (claude-code/codex/antigravity/etc. transcript dirs the daemon must enumerate on every fresh coexist-daemon cold start before it notices the ONE new vibe session dir) rather than a vibe-specific parser/classifier defect — a --attach recording against an already-warm daemon (this skill's normal flow) would likely not hit this discovery lag, but doing so would have required rebuilding and replacing the user's live production daemon, which this session avoided. Recording: replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/scenarios/1-3_long-idle-live-session/recordings/2026-07-13-13-09-03_irrlichd-0.5.7+2c97cfa (events at 13:09:03-13:09:59). daemon_capability stays bug; known_failing stays true pending investigation of the session-discovery-latency mechanism.

text: Session appears in ready at launch (pre-session proc-<PID> or the session_<ts> id, whichever arrives first)
First user prompt drives ready -> working
Text-only assistant reply (no tool_calls) settles turn_done back to ready
Session does NOT disappear past readyTTL; the pid>0 liveness skip holds the bound session in ready during the idle
Next user prompt after the idle: ready -> working resumes normally on the same session
Resume turn completes back to ready; final session state is ready
